title: Plantwide Overhead Rate
event: Reyes Co. budgets total overhead of $480,000 and 32,000 direct labor hours plantwide. Product A uses 1,200 direct labor hours. Compute the plantwide rate and the overhead allocated to Product A.

computationPaths.narration: One rate for the whole plant — simple, but it assumes every product consumes overhead in proportion to one single driver.

memorize.body: Plantwide rate = total budgeted OH ÷ total budgeted driver. Allocation = rate × the product's driver usage.
Plantwide rates overcost high-volume simple products and undercost low-volume complex ones — the distortion ABC exists to fix.

questions.prompt: Plantwide rate $15/DLH, Product A uses 1,200 DLH. Overhead allocated = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the rate. Allocation multiplies it by the product's 1,200 hours: 18,000.
